title: Stellar Wave 6 — Demo and testnet setup
goal: Make the whole Stellar thesis reproducible from scratch on Testnet with a single guided script and runbook, and wire the Stellar tools through the existing MCP proxy. The demo creates and funds a fresh Testnet account via Friendbot, configures native multisig so master/user weight + Compass key sum to the medium threshold (Compass required), then drives all six demo cases end-to-end printing decision, observable on-network outcome, and audit summary. No new brain logic, no proxy-core change, no on-chain contract — native Stellar multisig is the reproducibility win versus the Solana Anchor program.

context.precedent: docs/wave-8-demo-hardening/ (Solana demo hardening runbook + single-path reproducible demo); scripts/e2e-pipeline-test.mjs and scripts/test-user-flow.mjs (existing demo/orchestration script style)

openQuestions.question: Should the demo run against the existing MCP proxy end-to-end, or call the gateways directly for speed?
openQuestions.resolution: DECIDED: Default to running THROUGH the existing MCP proxy for fidelity (it exercises the real routed surface and proves the tools are listed without proxy-core changes). Offer a --gateway-direct flag as a faster fallback for local iteration. The proxy core is never modified.

openQuestions.question: How should the 'user' signature be represented in an automated demo?
openQuestions.resolution: DECIDED: Generate a second Testnet keypair that acts as the user/master signer, created and Friendbot-funded by the demo in the setup step. Compass's cosigner key comes from the Wave 4 signer configuration and is never generated by the demo, so the gate uses the real Compass key.

acceptanceCriteria: The demo is reproducible from a clean state via the documented planned script (scripts/stellar-demo.mjs).
A fresh Testnet account is created and funded via Friendbot using the Wave 1 helper.
The account's native multisig is configured so master/user weight + Compass key sum to the medium threshold, and neither signer alone reaches the threshold (Compass is a required signer).
inspectAccount (Wave 4) confirms the signers, weights, and thresholds before any case runs; the demo aborts if Compass is not genuinely required.
All six demo cases produce the expected decision and the expected observable on-network outcome.
Each of the six cases leaves an audit record (Wave 5).
The Stellar tools are listed via the existing MCP proxy without modifying the proxy core.
The brain (policy engine, LLM judge, sanitizer, COMPASS_DECISIONS) is untouched.
The Solana demo (docs/wave-8-demo-hardening/) still works unchanged.
No custom on-chain contract is deployed — the multisig guarantee is native Stellar.
nonGoals: No change to Solana — the Solana demo keeps working unchanged
No change to the brain (policy engine, LLM judge, sanitizer, COMPASS_DECISIONS)
No change to the MCP proxy core — Stellar tools are exposed THROUGH the chain-agnostic proxy
No new decision logic, policy, or risk classification — Wave 6 only orchestrates Wave 1-5 behavior
No mainnet readiness and no real-funds path — Testnet/Friendbot only
No on-chain contract deploy — native Stellar multisig is used
No hosted or CI-driven continuous demo, and no frontend/UI walkthrough
verification: Manual: run scripts/stellar-demo.mjs from a clean state and confirm all six cases match the expected decision and observable on-network outcome, each with an audit record
Manual: call tools/list through the existing MCP proxy and confirm the Stellar tools appear with no proxy-core diff
Manual: confirm inspectAccount reports signers/weights/thresholds such that Compass is required (user-only and Compass-only are below threshold)

tasks.title: Scaffold demo orchestrator and declarative six-case table
tasks.goal: Create the demo script scaffold modeled on scripts/e2e-pipeline-test.mjs with a declarative table of the six demo cases (envelope shape, userSigns, expectedDecision, expectedOutcome) and a verdict-table printer. No live calls yet.

tasks.title: Create and fund a fresh Testnet account via Friendbot
tasks.goal: Generate a user/master keypair and fund the account via the Wave 1 Friendbot helper against STELLAR_FRIENDBOT_URL; print the account id and confirm a positive XLM balance on Horizon. Use the real Compass cosigner key from the Wave 4 signer config.

tasks.title: Configure native multisig so Compass is required
tasks.goal: Submit a setOptions operation setting signer weights and low/medium/high thresholds so master/user weight + Compass key == medium threshold and neither signer alone reaches it. Then call inspectAccount (Wave 4) and assert Compass is required; abort with a clear error if not.

tasks.title: Drive all six cases end-to-end through the existing MCP proxy
tasks.goal: For each case: build the envelope, route through the existing MCP proxy (tools/list then tool call), let the unchanged brain decide, let Wave 4 cosign sign only on ALLOW, submit, and record the observable on-network outcome. Cover Case1 ALLOW/executes, Case2 DENY/not executable, Case3 ESCALATE out-of-range, Case4 ESCALATE critical op, Case5 user-only -> network rejects, Case6 user+Compass -> executable. Provide a --gateway-direct fallback that bypasses the proxy. Do not modify the proxy core.

tasks.title: Collect per-case audit records and print the verdict table
tasks.goal: For every case, capture the Wave 5 audit record id and print a verdict table (case id, expected vs observed decision, observable outcome, audit id). Exit non-zero if any case fails to match expectation.

tasks.title: Finalize runbook and validate clean-state reproducibility
tasks.goal: Ensure runbook.md matches the script behavior (prerequisites, env vars, numbered steps, expected outcome per step, native-multisig win note). Run the verification commands and a manual clean-state demo, and confirm the Solana demo and full backend suite still pass.
